concept: Assess / Evaluate Evidence Against a Stated Criterion
description: To evaluate evidence, a performance, or a condition against a stated criterion, for a stated purpose. An assessment can be formal or a glance across the workshop, and it is only a reading of the evidence: approving, certifying, and deciding consequences are separate acts with their own words.
sound_symbolism: Criterion-bearing 'ki' opens the evaluation, 'so' gathers the relevant evidence, and final 'me' holds attention on the particular object and purpose. The form is deliberate without suggesting final judgment.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'shia ha theko kisome.' (They assess the craft.) State the criterion, evidence, assessor, purpose, uncertainty, and consequences where relevant. The event-noun rule supplies assessment. Distinct from 'somela' (observe), 'nilaki' (inspect), 'somaki' (test), 'tukiro' (review), and 'kirema' (criterion).

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Explicit criteria and evidence make an assessment contestable and prevent a finding about one performance from becoming an unspoken judgment of the whole person.

tags.activity: evaluating a performance, state, or result
tags.cognition: comparing evidence with a stated criterion
